Header file for controlling vibration motor strength and state in firmware.

# Purpose
This C header file defines constants and function prototypes for controlling a vibration motor. It includes the necessary standard libraries `stdbool.h` and `stdint.h` for boolean and integer type definitions. The constants `VIBE_STRENGTH_MAX`, `VIBE_STRENGTH_MIN`, and `VIBE_STRENGTH_OFF` represent the maximum forward power, maximum reverse power, and the stopped state of the motor, respectively. The functions [`vibe_init`](<#vibe_init>), [`vibe_ctl`](<#vibe_ctl>), [`vibe_force_off`](<#vibe_force_off>), and [`vibe_set_strength`](<#vibe_set_strength>) provide an interface for initializing the motor, controlling its power state, forcing it off, and setting its strength. The function [`vibe_get_braking_strength`](<#vibe_get_braking_strength>) returns the strength needed to brake the motor to a stop.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdbool.h`
- `stdint.h`
